comments.text: Unless I’m missing something, Stephan Brandt’s bound $f(n;3)\le Cn$ in his paper "Expanding graphs and Ramsey numbers" already answers the $F(n)/n\to \infty$ question negatively. I was unable to find a copy of his paper online, so I reconstructed it based off a few fragments I found in university archives in LaTeX. In his paper he proves a linear bound of the form $[ f(n;3) \le C n ]$ (for example $f(n;3)/n<84$ for sufficiently large $n$). I think this implies $f(n)/n$ is bounded, so the question $f(n)/n\to\infty$ has a negative answer. Here is the reconstructed Latex code and compiled PDF of the Brandt’s paper. EDIT: Brandt used different notation where the universal Ramsey-goodness threshold f(n;3) is the same as the parameter F(n) in BEFRS80 if I am not mistaken. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
The .dvi and .ps files of Brandt's paper can be found here for reference.
Compiling the .ps file to PDF, resulted in a lot of OCR corruptions. So having a Latex rewrite is good if someone wants to try formalizing this in Lean.
